name: Spiced Garbanzos with Eggplant and Tomatoes
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Cabernet Sauvignon or reserved wine
8 ounces angel hair pasta, cooked, drained, hot
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut a whole eggplant into 1/3-inch slices (about 12 slices). Transfer the slices to a cookie sheet lined with paper towels and sprinkle with a little fresh ground coarse salt. Freeze for 2 to 4 hours.
2. Bag half of the frozen eggplant slices and return to the freezer. Cube the remaining slices for this recipe.
3. Prepare all ingredients ready for cooking, including the spice blend.
4. Heat a wok or large skillet over high heat. Spray with olive oil spray.
5. Add the cubed eggplant to the hot wok and sauté for 3 to 4 minutes until golden brown.
6. Sprinkle the spice blend evenly over the eggplant and warm it slightly before adding, then toss to coat.
7. In a separate well in the same pan, add half of the olive oil and sauté the chopped onion and minced garlic for 2 to 3 minutes until softened.
8. Add the remaining olive oil and bell peppers to the pan. Sauté for about 2 minutes until aromatic.
9. Stir in the cooked garbanzo beans and warm through.
10. Pour in the Cabernet Sauvignon or reserved wine and cook, uncovered, for about 1 minute to burn off alcohol.
11. Add the drained canned tomatoes and heat through; stir to combine with the tomato juice if desired.
12. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt, pepper, and other seasonings as needed.
13. Toss the cooked pasta with the vegetable mixture and serve with a sprinkle of parmesan cheese.
